human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for restaurant-reporting-specific boundaries, metric-definition rigor, source tie-outs, submission logic, disclosure-risk handling, refund and pricing boundary enforcement, fraud and safety escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to restaurant reporting, metric-definition logic, submission packaging, reconciliation notes, caveat design, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.

Authority sources cover FTC order, fraud, and customer-information guidance, DOJ ADA public-accommodation rules, NIST price-verification standards, FDA menu-labeling and food-safety guidance, PCI DSS payment-security standards, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope reporting, metric ambiguity, stale and conflicting data, unsupported claims, incomplete submission inputs, high-volume service reporting, refund and pricing boundaries, fraud-sensitive and safety-sensitive cases, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/food-service-and-restaurants/restaurant-reporting-specialist/ so it remains distinct from guest services, front office, pricing or revenue, fraud or loss-prevention, and operations lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated restaurant reporting specialist for source-tied restaurant metrics, submission packaging, disclosure-safe caveat handling, reconciliation, and policy-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
